MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Trustee and the Attorney to appear on October 16, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why the third report and account has not been filed. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-28-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Eliot Lippman for and with (trustee) Anne Smith.

The Court confirmed that they are not requesting fees and that fees will be reduced by interest, instructed counsel to prepare a new proposed order and stated that there is an issue with trustee fees for personal services and travel time. Mr. Lipman stated that the conservator was the Public Guardian, that Ms. Smith was appointed as trustee for her case management and social work skills, that Ms. Kearney wanted to remain at home and wanted Ms. Smith to act in full service, that they are asking for $8000.00 which is for 2 years of services, that Ms. Kearney required that Ms. Smith meet with her to explain what was going on and that in regards to the $400.00 for travel Ms. Smith is in San Mateo County and had to travel to San Francisco to meet with Ms. Kearny. Mr. Lipman further stated that Ms. Kearney was cash poor, that the trustee was authorized to obtain a reverse mortgage, that the bank required that she have Power of Attorney to sign the documents and that she has only provided services as a trustee not an agent. The Court stated that Ms. Smith's role was only as trustee of the trust. Mr. Lipman stated that there are no charges as an agent and that this was only used to obtain the loan. The Court took the accounting petition under submission.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-02-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Eliot Lippman for and with (trustee) Anne Smith.

The Court stated that this is an amended first account, that there are a few issues, that it appears that the conservatee's attorney was paid without a court order, that the accounting in the trust includes time for the Power of Attorney and that it should be separate, that there is substantial time and travel expenses for personal services when the status of Ms. Smith was trustee only and that additional accountings will be needed. Mr. Lippman stated that at the start of the matter Ms. Kearney lived alone, that she was subject to elder financial abuse, that the Public Guardian was appointed as conservator, that Nancy Field was the court appointed attorney and received payment, that a petition for substituted judgment establishing the trust was filed, that the conservatorship was terminated, that Ms. Kearney was able to manage her affairs but needed help, that they did not think the trust was under court supervision, that Ms. Kearney filed a petition for an amendment to the trust to appoint Anne Smith as trustee, that Ms. Field brought a petition for a reverse mortgage at the direction of Ms. Kearney, that Ms. Field prepared the Power of Attorney with Anne Smith as agent as that is what the lender required, that Ms. Field presented her invoice for working with Ms. Kearney directly, that they were not aware of the language in the conservatorship order requiring court confirmation on fees, that Mr. Lippman oversaw the receipt of assets from the Public Guardian, that the Public Guardian never submitted a final accounting, that this is what Ms. Kearney wanted, that Ms. Kearney personally interviewed Ms. Smith who is a Licensed Fiduciary and Social Worker, that this enabled Ms. Kearney to live in her home for an additional 6 years, that they believe that the work done by the trustee regarding personal care was reasonable and that they had brought the trust back to the court to have a referee appointed for the sale of the property. The Court stated that the question is whether it is appropriate to pay out of the trust. Mr. Lippman stated that in regards to paying fees now, the trustee is requesting fees for the initial 2 year period, that there are no fees requested for preparing the accounting yet, and that they ask that her fees be considered now. The Court stated that it does not question Ms. Field's services but that she knew she was not entitled to payment without a court order. Mr. Lippman asked the Court to approve what had been done and apologized for not following the Court order. The Accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Apr-18-2016 Continued To May-23-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Eliot Lippman for and with (trustee) Anne Smith.

The Court asked counsel to address the difference in the accountings between the end of the Public Guardian's accounting (3/6/08) and their accounting, that there is a difference in shares and value and that it needs to be accounted for. Mr. Lippman stated that this was a conservatorship and a trust, that the conservatorship was terminated as the conservatee had legal capacity, that Ms. Smith was named as successor trustee and that the Public Guardian was discharged. The Court clarified that a resignation was accepted that the Public Guardian was not discharged and that they still have a duty to provide information to the current trustee. Mr. Lippman stated that they have provided the court with everything that was provided to them. The Court stated that the current trustee must separate the petition regarding the trustee fees and her work as Power of Attorney. Mr. Lippman stated that they will make inquiries immediately but that he does not know how soon the Public Guardian will respond. The accounting petition was continued to May 23, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Order to Appear is off calendar.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

For Court Order Amending Trust To Change Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Feb-25-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joel Blackman for the San Francisco Public Guardian (SFPG). Nancy Fields for the Conservatee.

The Court stated that the conservatorship needed to be terminated, and that the SFPG'S first and final account was needed, and that the first and final account on both trusts were also needed. Mr. Blackman stated that he was not sure if any property from the old trust was transferred to the new trust, that he was ready to do a schedule for the first and final on the old trust, and that he would file a new petition to terminate by March 24, 2008. Ms. Fields stated that her client was very upset, that things were not moving fast enough, and that she would like to see the petition to terminate by March 10, 2008. The Court will allow counsel to determine who will file the petition to terminate. The Court approved the petition appointing Anne F. Smith the successor trustee. A hearing date of March 24, 2008 is set for status on the petition to terminate. and the accountings for all 3 three matters.

Judge Pro Tem: Sue Kaplan. Reporter: Maria Letora CSR 11935. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Hill.
